title: alnus incana subsp. hirsuta
intro: Alnus incana subsp. hirsuta, also known as the Hairy Alder, is a subspecies of tree belonging to the alder family. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of the Hairy Alder have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat infections. Alnus incana subsp. hirsuta can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, flu, and respiratory problems.
